quotes.text: The 1934 'Surgeon's Photograph' of Nessie was admitted, sixty years later, to be a toy submarine with a sculpted neck.
Roger Patterson's 1967 16mm reel of a striding Bigfoot has been re-analyzed dozens of times. The argument never ends.
From November 1966 to the Silver Bridge collapse in 1967, Point Pleasant, West Virginia reported over 100 Mothman sightings.
Eric Shipton's 1951 photo of a 13-inch footprint on Menlung Glacier remains the most famous Yeti evidence ever published.
Madelyne Tolentino's 1995 description of the original Puerto Rico Chupacabra was, she later admitted, lifted directly from the film Species.
In January 1909, hundreds of New Jersey residents across thirty towns reported seeing the Jersey Devil in a single week.
Lake Champlain's 'Champ' has been reported by lakeside residents since the 1880s — Vermont has a state law protecting it.
Florida's Skunk Ape was supposedly photographed in 2000 by an anonymous Sarasota grandmother — twice, in detail, never explained.
